# shaolin

> A standalone modular CQRS/Event-Sourcing backend framework for Ruby (4.0+). Each entity is a
> self-contained folder (`app/modules/<name>/`) with an enforced public contract. Commands mutate
> event-sourced aggregates; events persist to a Postgres event store; projections build read
> models you query. HTTP and RabbitMQ are interchangeable transports over the same buses; async
> side effects run via a transactional outbox + worker. Designed to
> be operated by AI agents: deterministic conventions, generators, explicit requires, machine-
> readable contracts.

## Build an app

- `shaolin new <app>`: scaffold a runnable app (Gemfile, config/boot.rb, bin/server, Dockerfile,
  Knative deploy/service.yaml, AGENTS.md).
- `shaolin g module <plural>`: scaffold a full module that boots immediately. Inflection:
  `orders` → namespace `Orders`, entity `Order`, command `CreateOrder`, event `OrderCreated`,
  read model table `orders_read`, event topic `orders.order_created`.
- `shaolin server` (Falcon), `shaolin console`, `shaolin migrate`, `shaolin routes`.
- **Agent tools:** `shaolin describe --json` (machine-readable map of all modules: commands,
  events, imports, exports — load this to understand the whole app), `shaolin schemas`
  (command/event surface), `shaolin lint` (isolation check — flags cross-module reach-ins, including
  in reactors), `shaolin graph` (module dependency graph), `shaolin projections rebuild [name]`
  (replay events into read models), `shaolin g module <name> --crud` (plain CRUD module, no event
  sourcing), `shaolin g module <name> --reactor` (also scaffold an async reactor + spec).
- **Async runtimes:** `shaolin worker` (process the outbox — runs reactors; `WORKER_CONCURRENCY=N`
  threads, retries with exponential backoff, dead-letter on exhaustion, graceful SIGTERM),
  `shaolin scheduler` (fire `Shaolin.schedule` tasks; single leader via a Postgres advisory lock).
  `describe --json` also lists each module's `reactors` (with subscribed events) and top-level
  `scheduled` tasks.

## Module anatomy (`app/modules/<name>/`)

- `module.rb` — manifest: `Shaolin.module "<name>" do imports/exports/commands_handled/events_published end`. The public boundary.
- `commands/<verb>_<entity>.rb` — `Shaolin::ValueObject` (typed intent).
- `events/<entity>_<pasttense>.rb` — `RubyEventStore::Event` subclass (domain event).
- `<entity>.rb` — aggregate: `include Shaolin::CQRS::Aggregate`; command methods call `apply(Event.new(data:))`; `on(Event) { |e| ... }` mutates replay state.
- `command_handlers/<...>_handler.rb` — `< Shaolin::CQRS::CommandHandler`; `handles SomeCommand`; `def call(cmd) ... end`; use `aggregate_repository.unit_of_work(Aggregate.new(id)) { |a| a.do }`; return `Dry::Monads Success/Failure`.
- `projections/<name>_projection.rb` — `< Shaolin::CQRS::Projection`; `on(Event) { |e| ReadModels::X.project(id: e.data[:id]) { |r| ... } }`.
- `read_models/<entity>_record.rb` — `< Shaolin::AR::ReadModel` (ActiveRecord), `self.table_name = "<name>_read"`.
- `queries/` — read the read models.
- `dto/<...>_dto.rb` — `< Shaolin::DTO`; `json { required(:x).filled(:string) }`; `.validate(params)` → `success?/failure?/to_h/errors`.
- `controllers/<name>_controller.rb` — `< Shaolin::HTTP::Controller`; `routes do post "/x", :create; get "/x/:id", :show end`; validate DTO → `command_bus.call(Command.new(...))` → `render_result(result, location:)`; reads via read models; `command_bus`/`query_bus`/`event_store` are available.
- `reactors/<entity>_reactor.rb` (optional) — `< Shaolin::Jobs::Reactor`; `on(Events::X) { |e| ... }`.
  Like a projection, but the handler runs LATER in `shaolin worker` via the outbox (NOT in the
  event's transaction). At-least-once delivery → handlers MUST be idempotent. Use for side effects:
  send email, publish to RabbitMQ (`Shaolin::Kernel["messaging.publisher"].publish(...)`), call an API.
- `db/migrate/*_create_<name>_read.rb` — read-model migration.
- `CONTRACT.md` — generated summary; read it to understand a module without its internals.

## Rules for an agent changing a module

1. Stay inside one module folder. Cross-module access only via declared `imports`/`exports`.
2. Reference sibling files with explicit `require_relative` (no autoloading).
3. To add a field: edit the command (attribute), event (data), DTO (rule), aggregate (apply),
   projection (write), and the read-model migration (column). Keep them consistent.
4. Domain invariants needing history live in the aggregate; shape/format checks live in the DTO.
5. Run `bundle exec rspec`. Verify HTTP with `shaolin server` + curl.

## Result → HTTP

`Success` → 200 (201 with `location:`); `Failure([:not_found,..])` → 404; `Failure([:conflict,..])`
→ 409; validation/other → 422. Bodies: `{ "error": { "code", "message" } }`.

## Async: reactors, worker, scheduler, RabbitMQ

- **Reactor** = an async event handler for side effects. Scaffold with `g module <name> --reactor`
  or add `reactors/<x>_reactor.rb` by hand. When its subscribed event is appended, the `:jobs`
  provider inserts an outbox row **in the same transaction** as the event (transactional outbox —
  no lost effect on a crash between commit and side effect).
- `shaolin worker` claims due rows (`FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`), loads the event, runs the reactor,
  marks done; on error it retries with exponential backoff `[1s,10s,60s,600s,3600s]` and dead-letters
  after exhausting attempts. At-least-once → **reactors must be idempotent**.
- `Shaolin.schedule("name", every: "5m") { command_bus.call(...) }` (in `config/` or a module) +
  `shaolin scheduler` fires periodic work; a Postgres advisory lock guarantees a single leader across
  replicas.
- **Cross-service (RabbitMQ):** a reactor publishes an `IntegrationEvent` via
  `Shaolin::Kernel["messaging.publisher"]` (swap the in-memory publisher for
  `Shaolin::RabbitMQ::Publisher` — same `Shaolin::Messaging::Publisher` port). Another service runs
  `Shaolin::RabbitMQ::Consumer.connect(queue:, routing_keys:).run { |env| command_bus.call(...) }`.
  Publishing through the outbox makes delivery reliable.

## Redis: cache, store, broker (`shaolin-redis`)

`Shaolin::Redis.register_provider!(url:, namespace:)` registers `redis.pool`, `redis.cache`,
`cache.store`, `redis.store`, `redis.publisher` in the kernel. All on `redis-rb` + connection_pool.
- **Cache** (`Shaolin::Redis::Cache`, implements the `Shaolin::Cache` port — swap with the in-memory
  `Shaolin::Cache::Memory`): `cache.fetch("k", ttl: 300) { compute }`, `write/read/delete/clear`.
  Server-side TTL (shared across processes); JSON values with symbol keys.
- **Store** (`Shaolin::Redis::Store` — Redis as a database): `set/get` (JSON), `increment` (native),
  `hset/hget/hgetall`, `keys(pattern)`. For read models, sessions, counters, LLM state. Source of
  truth (no implicit TTL), unlike the cache.
- **Broker — Streams** (reliable): `Shaolin::Redis::StreamPublisher` implements the same
  `Shaolin::Messaging::Publisher` port as RabbitMQ (publish via `XADD`); `StreamConsumer` uses
  consumer groups (`XREADGROUP`/`XACK`, `XAUTOCLAIM` to reclaim a crashed worker's entries) for
  at-least-once consumption. A reactor can publish to Redis Streams or RabbitMQ with zero code change.
- **Broker — Pub/Sub** (`Shaolin::Redis::PubSub`): fire-and-forget fan-out (no persistence/acks).

## Production notes

- **Atomic outbox (automatic).** With the `:active_record` provider, a command's unit of work runs in
  ONE DB transaction — event append + sync projections + outbox enqueue commit or roll back together.
  No manual `transaction` needed; a crash can't leave an event without its reactor job. Enqueue is
  idempotent (unique `(reactor, event_id)`), so re-publishing never duplicates a job.
- **Connection pool.** Set `DB_POOL` >= concurrent fibers/threads. `shaolin worker --threads N` needs
  `DB_POOL>=N` (it warns otherwise). Falcon is fiber-per-request → `shaolin server` sets `:fiber`
  isolation automatically; the worker stays `:thread`.
- **Migrations are a release step.** With `SHAOLIN_ENV=production`, boot does NOT auto-create schema or
  migrate; run `shaolin migrate` once per release (event-store + jobs schema + read-model migrations,
  advisory-locked so concurrent replicas don't race). In dev it's automatic.
- **HTTP limits/observability.** Request bodies are capped (`SHAOLIN_MAX_BODY_BYTES`, default 1 MiB →
  413). Every response carries `x-request-id` (propagated from an inbound header), and one structured
  JSON access-log line is emitted per request (`SHAOLIN_LOG=off` to silence).
- **Operating the queue.** `shaolin jobs stats` (counts by status), `shaolin jobs dead` (inspect
  dead-lettered), `shaolin jobs retry <id>` (re-queue after a fix).

## Reference

- Working example: `examples/demo` (a `users` module). Run `ruby examples/demo/verify.rb`.
- Reactor/outbox example: `examples/reactor` — `ruby examples/reactor/verify.rb` (publish event →
  outbox row → `worker.run_once` → reactor side effect).
- Redis example: `examples/redis` — `ruby examples/redis/verify.rb` (cache + store + Streams broker;
  needs a local `redis-server`).
- Design specs: `docs/superpowers/specs/`.
- Stack: dry-system, ruby_event_store + aggregate_root, ActiveRecord (Postgres), dry-validation/
  dry-struct, hanami-router, Falcon/Puma, Thor; jobs/outbox (PG `SKIP LOCKED` + advisory locks),
  messaging + RabbitMQ transport (bunny — pure Ruby); Redis cache/store/broker (redis-rb + Streams).
